arabic: كَتَبَ الشَّاعِرُ الْغَرِيبُ قَصِيدَةً عَنْ وَطَنِهِ الْبَعِيدِ. تَذَكَّرَ شَوَارِعَ مَدِينَتِهِ وَرَائِحَةَ الْمَطَرِ عَلَى التُّرَابِ. اشْتَاقَ إِلَى صَوْتِ أُمِّهِ وَإِلَى جِيرَانِهِ الطَّيِّبِينَ. فِي الْغُرْبَةِ، يُصْبِحُ كُلُّ شَيْءٍ صَغِيرٍ فِي الْوَطَنِ ذَكْرَى غَالِيَةً. قَالَ فِي قَصِيدَتِهِ: «الْوَطَنُ لَيْسَ أَرْضًا فَقَطْ، بَلْ هُوَ رَائِحَةٌ وَصَوْتٌ وَحُلُمٌ.»
translation: Pjesnik u tuđini napisao je pjesmu o svom dalekom domu. Sjećao se ulica svog grada i mirisa kiše na zemlji. Čeznuo je za glasom majke i za dobrim komšijama. U tuđini svaka sitnica iz domovine postaje dragocjena uspomena. U svojoj pjesmi je rekao: 'Domovina nije samo zemlja, ona je miris, glas i san.'
translationEn: The exiled poet wrote a poem about his distant homeland. He remembered the streets of his city and the smell of rain on the earth. He longed for his mother's voice and for his kind neighbours. In exile, every small thing from home becomes a precious memory. He wrote in his poem: 'The homeland is not just land — it is a scent, a voice, and a dream.'
